2.0 Violation Details

MEDIUM VIO-001 SBC Ref: 41-3.3.4

Visible flexible cable/cord run without concealed sections in damage-prone areas

Timestamp ⏱️ 00:04
Description Loose, unsecured wires dangling, increasing risk of tripping or damage.
SBC Code 41-3.3.4 — Flexible cables and cords in separated circuits shall be visible throughout any part of their length liable to mechanical damage.
Priority MEDIUM
MEDIUM VIO-002 SBC Ref: 54-3.8

PE conductor routing and physical protection

Timestamp ⏱️ 00:04
Description Loose, unsecured wires dangling, increasing risk of tripping or damage.
SBC Code 54-3.8 — Protective conductors shall be routed so as to minimize the possibility of damage or inadvertent disconnection during normal use or maintenance.
Priority MEDIUM
CRITICAL VIO-003 SBC Ref: 61-4.2.2k

Proper cable terminations (no loose/missing lugs)

Timestamp ⏱️ 00:04
Description Loose, unsecured wires dangling, increasing risk of tripping or damage.
SBC Code 61-4.2.2k — Initial verification inspection shall confirm: adequacy of termination and connection of cables and conductors (no loose wires, proper lug connections).
Priority CRITICAL
MEDIUM VIO-004 SBC Ref: 52-2.4

IP-rated enclosures where dust/solid foreign bodies present

Timestamp ⏱️ 00:06
Description Exposed wiring in ceiling cavity with no proper containment.
SBC Code 52-2.4 — Wiring systems in locations with presence of solid foreign bodies (AE) shall have appropriate degree of protection (IP code) marked on enclosures.
Priority MEDIUM
MEDIUM VIO-005 SBC Ref: 52-2.6

Mechanical protection on wiring in impact-prone areas

Timestamp ⏱️ 00:00
Description Open electrical wiring hanging from ceiling without protective enclosure (e.g., junction box, conduit).
SBC Code 52-2.6 — Wiring systems subject to impact (AG) shall be protected by location or by additional mechanical protection such as conduit, trunking, or armor.
Priority MEDIUM
